seoTitle: Clare vs Clarke Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Clare and Clarke Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Clare vs Clarke Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clare and Clarke Creek.
heading: Clare vs Clarke Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clare, Queensland and Clarke Creek, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clare has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Clarke Creek (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clare or Clarke Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clare has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Clarke Creek scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clare scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Clarke Creek scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clare or Clarke Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clare has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
